title: DNA to RNA: Transcription
script_summary: Exploring the crucial process of transcription that converts DNA into messenger RNA.

segments.voiceover: Genes are read into messenger RNA before proteins are built.
This process, called transcription, is the first step in protein synthesis.
But how does the DNA know what to say? It’s all in the sequence!
RNA polymerase reads the DNA strand and builds a complementary RNA strand.
This RNA strand carries the genetic instructions from the nucleus to the ribosomes.
And that's how your body reads its blueprints to create proteins!
Want to dive deeper into genetics? Hit that follow button!
